Home/Islands

Gulf of Naples

The Islands of the Gulf of Naples

Three islands, three completely different characters. Capri for glamour and iconic views. Ischia for volcanic spas and wild nature. Procida for authenticity and the Italy that tourists rarely find. All reachable by ferry or hydrofoil in less than an hour.

The most iconic

Capri

The Blue Grotto, the Faraglioni sea stacks and the glamorous Piazzetta: the most iconic island in the Gulf of Naples.

  • 40 min from Naples
  • No private cars
  • Blue Grotto
Discover Capri

Spas & Nature

Ischia

The green island of the Gulf: natural thermal parks, the Aragonese Castle, volcanic beaches and Monte Epomeo at 789 m.

  • 50 min from Naples
  • 100+ thermal springs
  • Aragonese Castle
Discover Ischia

The most authentic

Procida

The smallest and most authentic: colourful houses at Marina Corricella, Italian Capital of Culture 2022.

  • 15 min from Pozzuoli
  • Capital of Culture 2022
  • The Postman island
Discover Procida

Which Island is Right for You?

Each island has a distinct personality. Here's a quick comparison to help you choose.

Feature Capri Ischia Procida
Area 10.4 km² 46.3 km² 4.1 km²
Crowds Very high in summer Moderate Low to moderate
Price level €€€€ €€–€€€ €–€€
Best for Glamour, views, day trip 2–3 day stay, spas Authenticity, day trip
Beaches Rocks & pebbles Dark volcanic sand Dark & light sand
Can drive? No (residents only) Yes Scooters & small cars
From Naples ~40 min hydrofoil ~50 min hydrofoil ~35 min hydrofoil
Closest port Sorrento (25 min) Pozzuoli (35 min) Pozzuoli (15 min)

Getting to the Islands

All islands by ferry or hydrofoil

No bridges, no airports. The only way to reach the Gulf islands is by sea - which is itself part of the experience. Main departure points are Naples Molo Beverello and Pozzuoli. Sorrento connects to Capri only.

Naples → Capri

~40 min

Hydrofoil, year-round

Naples → Ischia

~50 min

Hydrofoil, year-round

Pozzuoli → Procida

~15 min

Ferry, year-round

Full ferry guide with timetables

Amalfi Coast Travel helps travelers from around the world plan their trip to the Amalfi Coast through guides, travel tips, and much more. The entire project is lovingly curated by Alessia, directly from Massa Lubrense.

The Amalfi Coast - A UNESCO World Heritage Site
